zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth de Ros於大約1320年出生在英國越可爾爾姆斯利時，她的父親William de Ros 2nd Baron de Ros36歲，母親Margery Badlesmere17歲。她於1337年7月16日與Sir William la Zouche在英國諾薩普頓史爾哈利沃斯結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1380年5月16日在英國諾薩普頓史爾哈利沃斯享年61歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾哈利沃斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth de Ros于大约1320年出生在英国越可爾爾姆斯利时，她的父亲William de Ros 2nd Baron de Ros36岁，母亲Margery Badlesmere17岁。她于1337年7月16日与Sir William la Zouche在英国諾薩普頓史爾哈利沃斯结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1380年5月16日在英国諾薩普頓史爾哈利沃斯享年61岁，埋葬在英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾哈利沃斯。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ros
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Enrique, Luis, Jose, Alberto, Eduardo, Rafael, Elena, Fidel, Joaquin, Manuel, Pablo, Ramon.  Southeast Asian Leang, Phon, Lap, Phan, Pho, Saroeun, Sophal, Sophan, Sophea, Tha, Thang, Yen.
Catalan: nickname for someone with blond hair or a fair complexion, ultimately from Latin russus.

variants.name: Rus
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Romanian Simion, Grigore, Doina, Dorel, Ilie, Liviu, Petru, Vasile.

Czech, Slovak, and Romanian (of Slavic origin): ethnic name for a Russian, ultimately derived from an Old Norse term meaning ‘oarsman, rower’ (from the root roths-), a reference to the fact that the Russian state was established by Scandinavians who rowed their ships up rivers inland from the Baltic sea. In some cases this may also be origin of the Slovenian and Croatian surname (see 3 below).
Czech: descriptive nickname from the adjective rusý ‘blond’.

variants.name: Ross
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from one or other of a number of Scottish and English places called Ross or Roos(e), especially Roose (Lancashire) and Roos (East Yorkshire). The placenames derive from a British ancestor of Welsh rhos ‘moor, heath, plain’, which is the sense of the cognate Gaelic word ros. The Scottish surname has at least three origins. First, the Anglo-Norman family from Roos (East Yorkshire) was introduced to Scotland when Robert of Roos, lord of Wark Castle (Northumberland), married Isabella, an illegitimate daughter of King William the Lion. Second, various families took the name from the province of Ross in northern Scotland and other places of that name. Third, there were Norman families in Scotland by the 13th century who probably derived their name from Rots in Normandy (see 2 below). The descendants of Godfrey de Ros, tenant of the de Morville lords of Cunninghame, were major landholders in Ayrshire, and almost certainly took their name from Rots. The Rose family of Kilravock (Nairnshire) may take their name from either of these three (see Rose ). The lairds of Balnagown adopted the surname Ross after the earldom of Ross (to which they considered themselves rightful heirs) had passed into other hands through the female line.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rots in Calvados (France), probably named with the ancient Germanic element rod ‘clearing’ (compare Rhodes ). This was the original home of a family de Ros, who were established in Kent in 1130 and had major estates in Kent until well into the 13th century.

English: habitational name from Wrose, in Shipley, near Bradford (Yorkshire), with re-spelling of Wr- as R- due to the loss of /w/ before /r/ in early modern English pronunciation. The spelling Wrose is no longer current. The placename derives from Old English wrāse ‘knot, something twisted’, referring to the steep-sided hill on which the settlement stands, with the sense ‘broken or twisting hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth de Ros was born about 1320, in Helmsley, Yorkshire, England, her father, William de Ros 2nd Baron de Ros, was 36 and her mother, Margery Badlesmere, was 17. She married Sir William la Zouche on 16 July 1337, in Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 16 May 1380, in Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England, at the age of 61, and was buried in Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth de Ros est née environ 1320, à Helmsley, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, William de Ros 2nd Baron de Ros, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Margery Badlesmere, avait 17 ans. Elle a épousé Sir William la Zouche le 16 juillet 1337, à Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 16 mai 1380, à Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 61 ans, et a été enterrée à Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth de Ros um 1320 in Helmsley, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William de Ros 2nd Baron de Ros 36 und ihre Mutter Margery Badlesmere 17. Sie hat am 16. Juli 1337 in Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England Sir William la Zouche geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 16. Mai 1380 im Alter von 61 Jahren in Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England und wurde in Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth de Ros è nata il circa 1320 nel Helmsley, Yorkshire, England, sua padre, William de Ros 2nd Baron de Ros, aveva 36 anni e sua madre, Margery Badlesmere, aveva 17 anni. Lei sposato Sir William la Zouche il 16 luglio 1337, nel Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 16 maggio 1380, nel Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England, all'età di 61 anni, e fu sepolta a Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Rosが約1320年にイギリスヨークシュアイアヘルムスリーで生まれた時，父William de Ros 2nd Baron de Rosは36歳，母Margery Badlesmereは17歳であった。1337年7月16日にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアハーリングウオルスでSir William la Zoucheと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1380年5月16日にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアハーリングウオルスで61歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアハーリングウオルスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Ros(이)가 대략 1320년 영국 요르크실르 헬슬리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William de Ros 2nd Baron de Ros(은)는 36세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margery Badlesmere(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 1337년 7월 16일 영국 노르섬프토실르 해링워스에서 Sir William la Zouche(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1380년 5월 16일 영국 노르섬프토실르 해링워스에서 61세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 해링워스에 묻혔다.

mn.Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England: Harringworth, North Hamtunscire, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth de Ros nasceu aproximadamente 1320, em Helmsley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William de Ros 2nd Baron de Ros, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Margery Badlesmere, tinha 17 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir William la Zouche em 16 de julho de 1337, em Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 16 de maio de 1380, em Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, com 61 anos, e foi sepultada em Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth de Ros родилась около 1320 г. в местности хелмсли, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени William de Ros 2nd Baron de Ros было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Margery Badlesmere, 17. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir William la Zouche 16 июля 1337 г. в местности харрингворт, нортамптоншир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она умерла 16 мая 1380 г. в местности харрингворт, нортамптоншир, Англия в возрасте 61 год и была похоронена в местности харрингворт, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth de Ros nació aproximadamente 1320, en Helmsley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, William de Ros 2nd Baron de Ros, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Margery Badlesmere, tenía 17 años. Se casó con Sir William la Zouche el 16 de julio de 1337, en Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 16 de mayo de 1380, en Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 61 años, y fue sepultada en Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
